title: SSH Private Key (Redacted at Import)
html:
TL;DR

  • On 2026-04-06 Yahir pasted a full OpenSSH private key into an Apple Note — the Apple Notes exporter correctly identified the credential and redacted the body at import per the vault's privacy sweep policy.
  • The note exists on disk as a pure stub with redacted: true frontmatter — no key material is recoverable from raw/.
  • Pairs with the April 14 ssh login note and the Rdudejr@gmail stub as evidence Yahir uses Apple Notes as a credentials junk drawer — exactly the antipattern the privacy sweep was built to catch.
  • Critical security hygiene artifact: stay-of-execution moment for what could have been an exposure event if the vault had ever been pushed to a public mirror.
  • Action implied: any key matching this note should be rotated; the original Apple Notes copy on Yahir's device is the only source of truth and is itself a soft-secret.

Notes

The raw file's body is a single line: [REDACTED: OpenSSH private key removed during privacy sweep on import]. The frontmatter retains redacted: true and redaction_note: "Original note contained an OpenSSH private key. Body redacted on import." — meaning the pipeline correctly flagged the -----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY----- header and dropped the payload before it reached raw/.

This is the second 2026 credential-paste incident captured in the vault, after the April 14 SSH login string (which contains the host fingerprint yahir@138.199.228.206 per the file slug — likely a Hetzner / VPS box). The pattern is now stable enough to call out: Yahir treats Apple Notes as a passwords / keys clipboard, not just a thinking surface.

In the , this is the secrets sub-drawer — alongside the IDA account number note, the Rdudejr@ stub, and the Tom Canchola contact as title note. Apple Notes' iCloud sync makes this a one-device-loss-from-exposure pattern. Worth promoting to a full hygiene synthesis page if a third paste-event surfaces.

Connections
